structural_block: the per-node symbolic build cubes polynomials past the NTT single-mul cap (~2^24 = 16777216 < 3^16 = 43046721); building even ONE node requires an unimplemented Kronecker/CRT multiplication path
projected_per_node_basis: measured resultant law ~c*deg^1.96 anchored at (deg 177147, 13.74s); evaluated at deg 3^16 (~182 core-hours/node)
caveat: Projection for the CURRENT stack only (non-amortized resultant; no Kronecker/CRT NTT for RP=10 degrees). NOT a lower bound on CICO RP=10 in general — an amortized-resultant elimination and/or Kronecker/CRT multiplication could change the cost class. This is a decision input for cluster spend under the implementation we actually have.
verdict: Under the current non-amortized, non-Kronecker/CRT stack: both the algebraic (3^22 nodes x ~182 core-hr/node) and brute-force routes are astronomically out of reach, and the algebraic build is not even feasible without Kronecker/CRT mul. The hard cost gate fails decisively for the current stack. (RP=10 is far beyond the RF=6/RP=6 reproduction wall of ~1.4e3 core-years measured in zt_gate_calibrate.)
reproduce: cargo run --release --bin cico_golden (degree law); cargo run --release --bin gate_calibrate (NTT cap note); cargo run --release --bin zt_gate_calibrate (resultant law)
